verses.dev: कृत्वैवमुदकं तस्मै प्रस्थितौ रामलक्ष्मणौ।अवेक्षन्तौ वने सीतां पश्चिमां जग्मतुर्दिशम्
verses.trans: Rama and Lakshmana offered libations (for Jatayu) and went westward, looking for Sita in the forest.

verses.dev: तौ दिशं दक्षिणां गत्वा शरचापासिधारिणौ।अविप्रहतमैक्ष्वाकौ पन्थानं प्रतिपेदतुः
verses.trans: Wielding bow, arrows and sword, both the princes of the Ikshvaku race went southward and entered an untrodden (densely wooded) tract.

words.m: two princes of the Ikshvaku race, (ताम् that)

verses.dev: गुल्मैर्वृक्षैश्च बहुभिर्लताभिश्च प्रवेष्टितम्।आवृतं सर्वतो दुर्गं गहनं घोरदर्शनम्
verses.trans: It was a dense, impenetrable and dreadful forest covered with shrubs, trees and creepers spread all over.

verses.dev: व्यतिक्रम्य तु वेगेन व्यालसिंहनिषेवितम्।सुभीमं तन्महारण्यं व्यतियातौ महाबलौ
verses.trans: The two mighty heroes swiftly forged through the great forest, which was an abode of serpents and lions.

verses.dev: ततः परं जनस्थानातत्रिक्रोशं गम्य राघवौ।क्रौञ्चारण्यं विविशतुर्गहनं तौ महौजसौ
verses.trans: Both the mighty princes of the Raghu dynasty covered a distance of three krosas from Janasthana and entered the impassable kraunchaforest.

verses.dev: नानामेघघनप्रख्यं प्रहृष्टमिव सर्वतः।नानापक्षिगणैर्युक्तं नानाव्यालमृगैर्युतम्दिदृक्षमाणौ वैदेहीं तद्वनं तौ विचिक्यतुः।तत्र तत्रावतिष्ठन्तौ सीताहरणकर्शितौ
verses.trans: Tormented due to abduction of Sita, the two princes anxious to see her searched all over the dense forest stopping here and there on the way. The thick forest looked like a conglomeration of big clouds. It was filled with different kinds of birds, snakes and animals.

words.m: both tormented  by the abduction of Sita
as if it was over joyed

verses.dev: नानामेघघनप्रख्यं प्रहृष्टमिव सर्वतः।नानापक्षिगणैर्युक्तं नानाव्यालमृगैर्युतम्दिदृक्षमाणौ वैदेहीं तद्वनं तौ विचिक्यतुः।तत्र तत्रावतिष्ठन्तौ सीताहरणकर्शितौ
verses.trans: Tormented due to abduction of Sita, the two princes anxious to see her searched all over the dense forest stopping here and there on the way. The thick forest looked like a conglomeration of big clouds. It was filled with different kinds of birds, snakes and animals.

words.m: both tormented  by the abduction of Sita
as if it was over joyed

verses.dev: ततः पूर्वेण तौ गत्वा त्रिक्रोशं भ्रातरौ तदा।क्रौञ्चारण्यमतिक्रम्य मतङ्गाश्रममन्तरादृष्ट्वा तु तद्वनं घोरं बहुभीममृगद्विजम्।ननासत्त्वसमाकीर्णं सर्वं गहनपादपम्ददृशाते तु तौ तत्र दरीं दशरथात्मजौ।पातालसमगम्भीरां तमसा नित्यसंवृताम्
verses.trans: Then both the sons of Dasaratha, went further east, crossed the kraunchaforest after three krosas. They saw a dreadful forest which was full of fierce animals and birds near the hermitage of sage Matanga. It was densely covered with trees. They saw there a cave perpetually enveloped in darkness which was as deep as the netherworld.

words.m: near the hermitage of sage Matanga
filled with fearful beasts and birds
teeming with different kinds of creatures

verses.dev: ततः पूर्वेण तौ गत्वा त्रिक्रोशं भ्रातरौ तदा।क्रौञ्चारण्यमतिक्रम्य मतङ्गाश्रममन्तरादृष्ट्वा तु तद्वनं घोरं बहुभीममृगद्विजम्।ननासत्त्वसमाकीर्णं सर्वं गहनपादपम्ददृशाते तु तौ तत्र दरीं दशरथात्मजौ।पातालसमगम्भीरां तमसा नित्यसंवृताम्
verses.trans: Then both the sons of Dasaratha, went further east, crossed the kraunchaforest after three krosas. They saw a dreadful forest which was full of fierce animals and birds near the hermitage of sage Matanga. It was densely covered with trees. They saw there a cave perpetually enveloped in darkness which was as deep as the netherworld.

words.m: near the hermitage of sage Matanga
filled with fearful beasts and birds
teeming with different kinds of creatures

verses.dev: ततः पूर्वेण तौ गत्वा त्रिक्रोशं भ्रातरौ तदा।क्रौञ्चारण्यमतिक्रम्य मतङ्गाश्रममन्तरादृष्ट्वा तु तद्वनं घोरं बहुभीममृगद्विजम्।ननासत्त्वसमाकीर्णं सर्वं गहनपादपम्ददृशाते तु तौ तत्र दरीं दशरथात्मजौ।पातालसमगम्भीरां तमसा नित्यसंवृताम्
verses.trans: Then both the sons of Dasaratha, went further east, crossed the kraunchaforest after three krosas. They saw a dreadful forest which was full of fierce animals and birds near the hermitage of sage Matanga. It was densely covered with trees. They saw there a cave perpetually enveloped in darkness which was as deep as the netherworld.

words.m: near the hermitage of sage Matanga
filled with fearful beasts and birds
teeming with different kinds of creatures

verses.dev: आसाद्य तौ नरव्याघ्रौ दर्यास्तस्याविदूरतः।ददृशाते महारूपां राक्षसीं विकृताननाम्
verses.trans: Both the princes,the best among men, reached the cave and saw a huge demoness with a disfigured face not far from the cave.

words.m: not very far from that place

verses.dev: भयदामल्पसत्त्वानां बीभत्सां रौद्रदर्शनाम्।लम्बोदरीं तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्रां करालीं परुषत्वचम्भक्षयन्तीं मृगान्भीमान्विकटां मुक्तमूर्धजाम्।प्रैक्षेतां तौ ततस्तत्र भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ
verses.trans: Then the two brothers saw a fearful demoness who could strike terror in the mind of the timid, an uncouth figure with huge curved skins, with a big stomach, dishevelled hair, sharp and long teeth, devouring wild animals.

words.m: a woman with hair spread out

verses.dev: भयदामल्पसत्त्वानां बीभत्सां रौद्रदर्शनाम्।लम्बोदरीं तीक्ष्णदंष्ट्रां करालीं परुषत्वचम्भक्षयन्तीं मृगान्भीमान्विकटां मुक्तमूर्धजाम्।प्रैक्षेतां तौ ततस्तत्र भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ
verses.trans: Then the two brothers saw a fearful demoness who could strike terror in the mind of the timid, an uncouth figure with huge curved skins, with a big stomach, dishevelled hair, sharp and long teeth, devouring wild animals.

words.m: a woman with hair spread out

verses.dev: सा समासाद्य तौ वीरौ व्रजन्तं भ्रातुरग्रतः।एहि रंस्यावहेत्युक्त्वा समालम्बत लक्ष्मणम्
verses.trans: She came close to them, held Lakshmana who was walking ahead of his brother and said Come, let us enjoy.

words.m: we will both sport and enjoy sex

verses.dev: उवाच चैनं वचनं सौमित्रिमुपगूह्य सा।अहं त्वयोमुखी नाम लब्धा ते त्वमसि प्रियःनाथ पर्वतकूटेषु नदीनां पुलिनेषु च।आयुश्शेषमिमं वीर त्वं मया सह रंस्यसे
verses.trans: Taking hold of Saumitri the demoness said 'I am Ayomukhi and I have been obtained by you. You got me, O hero, O lord For the rest of your life you will sport with me on the lofty hills and river banks.'

verses.dev: उवाच चैनं वचनं सौमित्रिमुपगूह्य सा।अहं त्वयोमुखी नाम लब्धा ते त्वमसि प्रियःनाथ पर्वतकूटेषु नदीनां पुलिनेषु च।आयुश्शेषमिमं वीर त्वं मया सह रंस्यसे
verses.trans: Taking hold of Saumitri the demoness said 'I am Ayomukhi and I have been obtained by you. You got me, O hero, O lord For the rest of your life you will sport with me on the lofty hills and river banks.'

verses.dev: एवमुक्तस्तु कुपितः खड्गमुद्धृत्य लक्ष्मणः।कर्णनासास्तनं तस्या निचकर्तारिसूदनः
verses.trans: Thus addressed, Lakshmana, subduer of enemies, became angry, took out the sword and cut off her ears, nose and breasts.

verses.dev: कर्णनासे निकृत्ते तु विस्वरं सा विनद्य च।यथागतं प्रदुद्राव राक्षसी भीमदर्शना
verses.trans: When the ears and nose of the frightening demoness were cut off she shouted in a fierce tone and ran away in the direction she had come from.

words.m: in the same way as she came

verses.dev: तस्यां गतायां गहनं व्रजन्तौ वनमोजसा।आसेदतुरमित्रघ्नौ भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौ
verses.trans: The destroyers of enemies, Rama and Lakshmana of great valour went through the dense forest after she ran away.

verses.dev: लक्ष्मणस्तु महातेजास्सत्त्ववान् शीलवान् शुचिः।अब्रवीत्प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यं भ्रातरं दीप्ततेजसम्
verses.trans: Lakshmana of great brilliance and power, of good conduct and pure character spoke to his highly effulgent brother with folded hands:

verses.dev: स्पन्दते च दृढं बाहुरुद्विग्नमिव मे मनः।प्रायशश्चाप्यनिष्टानि निमित्तान्युपलक्षये
verses.trans: My arm is throbbing heavily. My mind is agitated. The omens I see foretell some undesirable events.

verses.dev: तस्मात्सज्जीभवार्य त्वं कुरुष्व वचनं हितम्।ममैव हि निमित्तानि सद्यश्शंसन्ति सम्भ्रमम्
verses.trans: O noble brother be alert. Heed my words of good advice. The bad omens I see presage perils.

verses.dev: एष वञ्चुलको नाम पक्षी परमदारुणः।आवयोर्विजयं युद्धे शंसन्निव विनर्दति
verses.trans: This vanchulaka bird's loud and dreadful screams suggest our triumph in war.

verses.dev: तयोरन्वेषतोरेवं सर्वं तद्वनमोजसा।संजज्ञे विपुलः शब्दो प्रभञ्जन्निव तद्वनम्
verses.trans: As both the brothers with great prowess were searching for Sita all over, a huge sound exploded as if shattering the forest.

verses.dev: संवेष्टितमिवात्यर्थं गगनं मातरिश्वना।वनस्य तस्य शब्दोऽभूद्दिवमापूरयन्निव
verses.trans: It appeared as though a fierce wind rose from the forest and filled the sky.

verses.dev: तं शब्दं काङ्क्षमाणस्तु रामः कक्षे सहानुजः।ददर्श सुमहाकायं राक्षसं विपुलोदरम्
verses.trans: While looking for the sound, Rama with his brother saw a demon with a huge body and a big belly.

verses.dev: आसेदतुस्ततस्तत्र तावुभौ प्रमुखे स्थितम्।विवृद्धमशिरोग्रीवं कबन्धमुदरे मुखम्
verses.trans: Then both the brothers saw a very tall figure with a trunk that stood facing them. He had neither neck nor head. His face was in the stomach.

words.m: with his face in the stomach

verses.dev: रोमभिर्निचितैस्तीक्ष्णैर्महागिरिमिवोछ्रितम्।नीलमेघनिभं रौद्रं मेघस्तनितनिस्वनम्
verses.trans: With sharp bristled hair all over the body, he was a mammoth figure who looked like a mountain resembling a dark cloud with his voice like the thunder.

words.m: by those spread all over the body
voice like the thunder of the cloud.

verses.dev: अग्निज्वालानिकाशेन ललाटस्थेन दीप्यता।महापक्ष्मेण पिङ्गेन विपुलेनायतेन चएकेनोरसि घोरेण नयनेनाशुदर्शिना।महादंष्ट्रोपपन्नं तल्लेलिहानं महामुखम्
verses.trans: He had a single eye that was reddishbrown in colour resembling flaming fire. It was fixed in the chest. His eyelashes glowed. He had instant vision with massive fangs in his huge mouth. He was licking his lips.

verses.dev: अग्निज्वालानिकाशेन ललाटस्थेन दीप्यता।महापक्ष्मेण पिङ्गेन विपुलेनायतेन चएकेनोरसि घोरेण नयनेनाशुदर्शिना।महादंष्ट्रोपपन्नं तल्लेलिहानं महामुखम्
verses.trans: He had a single eye that was reddishbrown in colour resembling flaming fire. It was fixed in the chest. His eyelashes glowed. He had instant vision with massive fangs in his huge mouth. He was licking his lips.

verses.dev: भक्षयन्तं महाघोरानृक्षसिंहमृगद्विपान्।घोरौ भुजौ विकुर्वाणमुभौ योजनमायतौ
verses.trans: The terrific demon kept eating bears, lions, deer and elephants, catching with both his arms stretching up to one yojana.

verses.dev: कराभ्यां विविधान्गृह्यऋक्षान्पक्षिगणान्मृगान्।आकर्षन्तं विकर्षन्तमनेकान्मृगयूथपान्स्थितमावृत्य पन्थानं तयोर्भ्रात्रोः प्रपन्नयोः।
verses.trans: He stood rooted there, catching bears, flocks of birds and deer with both his hands stretching up to one yojana, pulling and pushing them. (Now) he obstructed the path of the two brothers.

verses.dev: अथ तौ समभिक्रम्य क्रोशमात्रे ददर्शतुःमहान्तं दारुणं भीमं कबन्धं भुजसंवृम्।कबन्धमिव संस्थानादतिघोरप्रदर्शनम्
verses.trans: Then the brothers after covering one krosa (2 miles) saw a cruel, fierce, mighty monster named Kabandha looking like a trunk. He had a dreadful appearance and his arms encircled the area.

words.m: at a distance of one krosa (2 miles)

verses.dev: अथ तौ समभिक्रम्य क्रोशमात्रे ददर्शतुःमहान्तं दारुणं भीमं कबन्धं भुजसंवृम्।कबन्धमिव संस्थानादतिघोरप्रदर्शनम्
verses.trans: Then the brothers after covering one krosa (2 miles) saw a cruel, fierce, mighty monster named Kabandha looking like a trunk. He had a dreadful appearance and his arms encircled the area.

words.m: at a distance of one krosa (2 miles)

verses.dev: स महाबाहुरत्यर्थं प्रसार्य विपुलौ भुजौ।जग्राह सहितावेव राघवौ पीडयन्बलात्
verses.trans: The longarmed demon spread his hands and caught hold of Rama and Lakshmana together by pressing them with all his force.

verses.dev: खड्गिनौ दृढधन्वानौ तिग्मतेजोवपुर्धरौ। भ्रातरौ विवशं प्राप्तौ कृष्यमाणौ महाबलौ
verses.trans: The two mighty brothers who wielded swords and strong bows and whose bodies were of fiery splendour became helpless, seized by the hands of Kabandha.

verses.dev: तत्र धैर्येण शूरस्तु राघवो नैव विव्यथे।बाल्यादनाश्रयत्वाच्च लक्ष्मणस्त्वतिविव्यथेउवाच स विषण्णस्सन्राघवं राघवानुजः।
verses.trans: Of the two heroes, Rama being courageous was not worried but Lakshmana being younger and not having as much courage, was very much concerned and depressed. He said to Rama:

verses.dev: पश्य मां वीर विवशं राक्षसस्य वशं गतम्मयैकेन विनिर्युक्तः परिमुञ्चस्व राघव।
verses.trans: O heroic Rama see how helpless I am under the control of the demon. Set yourself free, leaving me alone and using me as an offering.

words.m: under the control of the demon

verses.dev: मां हि भूतबलिं दत्वा पलायस्व यथासुखम्अधिगन्तासि वैदेहीमचिरेणेति मे मतिः।
verses.trans: Giving me to this monstor as an offering, you may run away happily. I think you will soon obtain Vaidehi.

verses.dev: प्रतिलभ्य च काकुत्स्थ पितृपैतामहीं महीम्तत्र मां राम राज्यस्थस्स्मर्तुमर्हसि सर्वदा।
verses.trans: O scion of the Kakutstha dynasty, on getting the hereditary kingdom and ruling over it you should always remember me.

verses.dev: लक्ष्मणेनैवमुक्तस्तु रामस्सौमित्रिमब्रवीत्मा स्म त्रासं कृथा वीर न हि त्वादृग्विषीदति।
verses.trans: O valiant Lakshmana, a hero like you should not get desparate. said Rama to Saumitri when he was thus entreated by Lakshmana.

verses.dev: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे क्रूरो भ्रातरौ रामलक्ष्मणौपप्रच्छ घननिर्घोषः कबन्धो दानवोत्तमः।
verses.trans: In the meanwhile the foremost of the demons, Kabandha, rumbling like thunder questioned the brothers, Rama and Lakshmana:

verses.dev: कौ युवां वृषभस्कन्धौ महाखड्गधनुर्धरौघोरं देशमिमं प्राप्तौ मम भक्षावुपस्थितौ।
verses.trans: Who are you with bulllike shoulders, wielding big swords, bows and arrows? By coming to this place you have become my food.

words.m: whose shoulders are comparable to a bull
both holding great sword and bow

verses.dev: वदतं कार्यमिह वां किमर्थं चागतौ युवाम्इमं देशमनुप्राप्तौ क्षुधार्तस्येह तिष्ठतः।
verses.trans: For what purpose have you come? What work have you here ? You have come at the right time as my food, since I am hungry.

verses.dev: सबाणचापखड्गौ च तीक्ष्णशृङ्गाविवर्षभौममास्यमनुसम्प्राप्तौ दुर्लभं जीवितं पुनः।
verses.trans: Holding bows, arrows and swords, looking like bulls with pointed horns you have entered my mouth. It is impossible for you to live any more.

words.m: holding bows and arrows and swords

verses.dev: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा कबन्धस्य दुरात्मनःउवाच लक्ष्मणं रामो मुखेन परिशुष्यता।
verses.trans: On hearing the words of the evilminded Kabandha, Rama said to Lakshmana with dried up mouth:

verses.dev: कृच्छ्रात् कृच्छ्रतरं प्राप्य दारुणं सत्यविक्रमःव्यसनं जीवितान्ताय प्राप्तमप्राप्य तां प्रियाम्।
verses.trans: O Lakshmana, your valour is truth.We have gone through from one calamity to a greater one without finding the beloved Sita. Now we have landed into a terrible crisis which will put an end to our own life.

verses.dev: कालस्य सुमहद्वीर्यं सर्वभूतेषु लक्ष्मणत्वां च मां च नरव्याघ्र व्यसनैः पश्य मोहितौ।
verses.trans: O tiger among men see how powerful the might of time is for all beings Just see yourself and myself afflicted wth this great calamity.

verses.dev: नातिभारोस्ति कालस्य सर्वभूतेषु लक्ष्मणशूराश्च बलवन्तश्च कृतास्त्राश्च रणाजिरे।कालाभिपन्नास्सीदन्ति यथा वालुकसेतवः
verses.trans: O Lakshmana look at the power of time over all creatures There is nothing too heavy for time. Even those who know the use of arms in war, or are brave or strong enough, cannot withstand the force of time like the barriers built with sand.

words.m: those who know the use of arms in the war

verses.dev: नातिभारोस्ति कालस्य सर्वभूतेषु लक्ष्मणशूराश्च बलवन्तश्च कृतास्त्राश्च रणाजिरे।कालाभिपन्नास्सीदन्ति यथा वालुकसेतवः
verses.trans: O Lakshmana look at the power of time over all creatures There is nothing too heavy for time. Even those who know the use of arms in war, or are brave or strong enough, cannot withstand the force of time like the barriers built with sand.

words.m: those who know the use of arms in the war

verses.dev: इति ब्रुवाणो दृढसत्यविक्रमो महायशा दाशरथिः प्रतापवान्।अवेक्ष्य सौमित्रिमुदग्रपौरुषं स्थिरां तदा स्वां मतिमात्मनाऽकरोत्
verses.trans: Firm in the strength of truth, famous and powerful Rama, son of Dasaratha, determined to act with a steadfast mind, spoke to Lakshmana of mighty manliness on seeing him. इत्यार्ष श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे एकोनसप्ततितमस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the sixtyninth sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

words.m: Firm in the strength of truth
